Title: IWS - Verify hostname can be changed via QPM
Preconditions: 1. Open Q-SYS Peripheral Manager (QPM) in a web browser.

Title: 2. IWS device is commissioned and accessible.
3. Log in to QPM with valid credentials.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to the IWS device in QPM.

Title: 2. Locate the hostname field.
3. Change the hostname to a new value (e.g., "IWS-LobbyA").
4. Save/apply the change.
5. Verify the hostname update takes effect.
Preconditions: 1. The hostname field is editable.

Title: 2. The new hostname is accepted and saved.
3. The device is accessible by its new hostname after the change.
4. Q-SYS Designer reflects the updated hostname.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify network settings can be configured via QPM
Preconditions: 1. Open Q-SYS Peripheral Manager (QPM) in a web browser.

Title: 2. IWS device is commissioned and accessible.
3. Log in to QPM with valid credentials.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to the IWS device network settings in QPM.

Title: 2. Configure a static IP address, netmask, and gateway.
3. Save/apply the changes.
4. Verify the device is reachable at the new IP address.
5. Optionally configure a static route and verify connectivity.
Preconditions: 1. IP address, netmask, and gateway fields are editable.

Title: 2. Changes are accepted and applied.
3. The device is reachable at the new network configuration.
4. Static routes, if configured, function correctly.
5. Q-SYS Designer can still communicate with the device at the new address.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify NTP settings can be configured via QPM
Preconditions: 1. Open Q-SYS Peripheral Manager (QPM) in a web browser.

Title: 2. IWS device is commissioned and accessible.
3. Log in to QPM with valid credentials.
4. An NTP server is available on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to the IWS device NTP settings in QPM.

Title: 2. Configure the NTP server address.
3. Save/apply the changes.
4. Verify the device synchronizes time with the configured NTP server.
Preconditions: 1. The NTP server field is editable.

Title: 2. The configuration is accepted and saved.
3. The device clock synchronizes with the NTP server.
4. Time synchronization status is visible in QPM.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify device certificate management via QPM
Preconditions: 1. Open Q-SYS Peripheral Manager (QPM) in a web browser.

Title: 2. IWS device is commissioned and accessible.
3. Log in to QPM with valid credentials.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to the IWS device certificate management section in QPM.

Title: 2. Generate a Certificate Signing Request (CSR).
3. Verify the CSR is generated successfully.
4. Install a signed certificate on the device.
5. Verify the certificate is active.
Preconditions: 1. CSR generation completes without error.

Title: 2. The CSR contains correct device information.
3. A signed certificate can be installed successfully.
4. The device uses the installed certificate for secure communication.
5. Certificate status is visible in QPM.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify secure communication management via QPM
Preconditions: 1. Open Q-SYS Peripheral Manager (QPM) in a web browser.

Title: 2. IWS device is commissioned and accessible.
3. Log in to QPM with valid credentials.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to the secure communication management section in QPM.

Title: 2. Verify TOFU (Trust On First Use) behavior for initial connection.
3. Verify the device appears in the trusted devices list after acceptance.
4. Move a device to the declined list and verify communication is blocked.
5. Verify rollover behavior when a device certificate changes.
Preconditions: 1. New devices appear in the pending list on first connection.

Title: 2. Accepting a device moves it to the trusted list and enables communication.
3. Declining a device blocks communication.
4. Certificate rollover is handled gracefully without manual intervention (or with appropriate prompts).
5. Trusted, pending, and declined lists are correctly maintained.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify 802.1X management via QPM
Preconditions: 1. Open Q-SYS Peripheral Manager (QPM) in a web browser.

Title: 2. IWS device is commissioned and accessible.
3. Log in to QPM with valid credentials.
4. An 802.1X-capable switch and RADIUS server are available on the network.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to the 802.1X settings in QPM for the IWS device.

Title: 2. Enable 802.1X port-based authentication.
3. Configure the EAP method and credentials.
4. Save/apply the changes.
5. Verify the device authenticates successfully on the switch port.
Preconditions: 1. 802.1X settings are configurable in QPM.

Title: 2. The device successfully authenticates to the RADIUS server.
3. The switch port transitions to an authorized state.
4. Network communication continues normally after authentication.
5. Authentication status is visible in QPM.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify security policy management via QPM
Preconditions: 1. Open Q-SYS Peripheral Manager (QPM) in a web browser.

Title: 2. IWS device is commissioned and accessible.
3. Log in to QPM with valid credentials.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to the security policy settings in QPM for the IWS device.

Title: 2. Configure password requirements (minimum length, complexity, etc.).
3. Configure session settings (timeout, maximum sessions, etc.).
4. Save/apply the changes.
5. Verify the new policies are enforced.
Preconditions: 1. Password requirement settings are editable and saved.

Title: 2. Session settings are editable and saved.
3. A password that does not meet requirements is rejected on next password change.
4. Sessions time out according to the configured policy.
5. Policy changes persist across device reboots.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify system log and state download via QPM
Preconditions: 1. Open Q-SYS Peripheral Manager (QPM) in a web browser.

Title: 2. IWS device is commissioned and accessible.
3. Log in to QPM with valid credentials.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to the system log/diagnostics section in QPM for the IWS device.

Title: 2. Initiate a system log download.
3. Initiate a system state download.
4. Verify the downloaded files.
Preconditions: 1. System log downloads successfully as a readable file.

Title: 2. System state downloads successfully.
3. The log contains relevant device events and timestamps.
4. The system state file contains current device configuration and status information.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify device reboot via QPM
Preconditions: 1. Open Q-SYS Peripheral Manager (QPM) in a web browser.

Title: 2. IWS device is commissioned and accessible.
3. Log in to QPM with valid credentials.
4. Design is deployed and IWS device is in "OK" state.
Preconditions: 1. Navigate to the device management section in QPM for the IWS device.

Title: 2. Initiate a device reboot.
3. Confirm the reboot if prompted.
4. Wait for the device to come back online.
5. Verify device status in QPM and Q-SYS Designer.
Preconditions: 1. The reboot command is accepted.

Title: 2. The device goes offline temporarily.
3. The device comes back online within a reasonable time.
4. The device returns to "OK" state in Q-SYS Designer without manual intervention.
5. All previous configuration is retained after reboot.
Preconditions: Manual

Title: IWS - Verify QPM re-authentication after password change
Preconditions: 1. Open Q-SYS Peripheral Manager (QPM) in a web browser.

Title: 2. IWS device is commissioned and accessible.
3. Log in to QPM with valid credentials.
Preconditions: 1. Change the IWS device password via QPM or device web interface.

Title: 2. Attempt to access the device in QPM after the password change.
3. Observe the authentication prompt.
Preconditions: 1. QPM prompts the user to re-enter credentials after the password change.

Title: 2. Entering the new password restores access to the device.
3. No authentication faults are shown in Configurator (Configurator does not authenticate).
4. The device remains functional and connected to the Core.
Preconditions: Manual
